Functions for managing real-time clock (RTC) time, timezone settings, and backup registers.

# Purpose
This code provides functionality for managing real-time clock (RTC) operations, specifically focusing on time and timezone management. It includes functions to sanitize and adjust time structures (`struct tm`) and `time_t` values to ensure compatibility with hardware constraints, such as the 2038 problem and the limitation of storing only two-digit years. The code also provides functions to retrieve and format the current time, convert `time_t` values to string representations, and manage timezone information using backup registers.

The code interacts with hardware components through functions that read from and write to RTC backup registers, allowing it to store and retrieve timezone data. It includes functions to set, get, and clear timezone information, as well as to check if a timezone is set. The code is compatible with STM32 microcontroller families, as indicated by the defined macros (`STM32F2_COMPATIBLE`, `STM32F4_COMPATIBLE`, `STM32F7_COMPATIBLE`). It includes headers for RTC, GPIO, power management, and system logging, indicating its integration with broader system functionalities.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `rtc_private.h`
- `drivers/rtc.h`
- `drivers/gpio.h`
- `drivers/pwr.h`
- `system/bootbits.h`
- `system/logging.h`
- `system/reset.h`
- `stdio.h`
- `mcu.h`


# Functions

### rtc\_sanitize\_struct\_tm<!-- {{#callable:rtc_sanitize_struct_tm}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/drivers/rtc_common.c#L33>)

Adjusts the `tm_year` field of a `struct tm` to ensure it falls within the range of 2000 to 2037.
- **Inputs**:
    - `t`: A pointer to a `struct tm` that contains the time information to be sanitized.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `t->tm_year` is less than 100 (which corresponds to the year 2000).
    - If `t->tm_year` is less than 100, set `t->tm_year` to 100 and return `true`.
    - Check if `t->tm_year` is greater than 137 (which corresponds to the year 2037).
    - If `t->tm_year` is greater than 137, set `t->tm_year` to 137 and return `true`.
    - If `t->tm_year` is within the range 100 to 137, return `false`.
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the `tm_year` was adjusted, otherwise returns `false`.


---
### rtc\_sanitize\_time\_t<!-- {{#callable:rtc_sanitize_time_t}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/drivers/rtc_common.c#L49>)

Sanitizes a `time_t` value by adjusting its year to fit within a specific range and updates the original `time_t` value.
- **Inputs**:
    - `t`: A pointer to a `time_t` value that needs sanitization.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Convert the `time_t` value pointed by `t` to a `struct tm` using [`gmtime_r`](<../util/time/time.c.md#gmtime_r>).
    - Call [`rtc_sanitize_struct_tm`](<#rtc_sanitize_struct_tm>) to adjust the year in the `struct tm` to be within the range of 2000 to 2037.
    - Convert the sanitized `struct tm` back to `time_t` using [`mktime`](<../util/time/mktime.c.md#mktime>) and update the original `time_t` value pointed by `t`.
    - Return the result of [`rtc_sanitize_struct_tm`](<#rtc_sanitize_struct_tm>), indicating if the year was adjusted.
- **Output**: A boolean value indicating whether the year in the `time_t` value was adjusted.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`gmtime_r`](<../util/time/time.c.md#gmtime_r>)
    - [`rtc_sanitize_struct_tm`](<#rtc_sanitize_struct_tm>)
    - [`mktime`](<../util/time/mktime.c.md#mktime>)

### rtc\_get\_time\_tm<!-- {{#callable:rtc_get_time_tm}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/drivers/rtc_common.c#L59>)

Converts the current RTC time to a `struct tm` format.
- **Inputs**:
    - `time_tm`: A pointer to a `struct tm` where the converted time will be stored.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call `rtc_get_time()` to get the current time as a `time_t` value.
    - Use `localtime_r()` to convert the `time_t` value to a `struct tm` and store it in the provided `time_tm` pointer.
- **Output**: No return value; the result is stored in the `struct tm` pointed to by `time_tm`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`localtime_r`](<../util/time/time.c.md#localtime_r>)


---
### rtc\_get\_time\_string<!-- {{#callable:rtc_get_time_string}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/drivers/rtc_common.c#L64>)

Converts the current RTC time to a string format and stores it in a provided buffer.
- **Inputs**:
    - `buffer`: A character array where the function will store the formatted time string.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `rtc_get_time()` to retrieve the current time as a `time_t` value.
    - Passes the `time_t` value and the `buffer` to `time_t_to_string()` to convert the time to a string format.
    - Returns the `buffer` containing the formatted time string.
- **Output**: A pointer to the `buffer` containing the formatted time string.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`time_t_to_string`](<#time_t_to_string>)


---
### time\_t\_to\_string<!-- {{#callable:time_t_to_string}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/drivers/rtc_common.c#L68>)

Converts a `time_t` value to a formatted string representation of local time.
- **Inputs**:
    - `buffer`: A character array where the formatted time string will be stored.
    - `t`: A `time_t` value representing the time to convert.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Convert the `time_t` value `t` to a `struct tm` representing local time using [`localtime_r`](<../util/time/time.c.md#localtime_r>).
    - Format the `struct tm` into a string using [`strftime`](<../applib/pbl_std/strftime.c.md#strftime>) with the format specifier `%c`, storing the result in `buffer`.
    - Return the `buffer` containing the formatted time string.
- **Output**: A pointer to the `buffer` containing the formatted time string.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`localtime_r`](<../util/time/time.c.md#localtime_r>)
    - [`strftime`](<../applib/pbl_std/strftime.c.md#strftime>)

### rtc\_set\_timezone<!-- {{#callable:rtc_set_timezone}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/drivers/rtc_common.c#L80>)

Stores timezone information into RTC backup registers.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``tzinfo``: A pointer to a `TimezoneInfo` structure containing timezone data to store.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Cast `tzinfo` to a `uint32_t` pointer `raw`.
    - Check that the size of `TimezoneInfo` does not exceed 5 `uint32_t` values using `_Static_assert`.
    - Write `raw[0]` to the `RTC_TIMEZONE_ABBR_START` backup register.
    - Write `raw[1]` to the `RTC_TIMEZONE_ABBR_END_TZID_DSTID` backup register.
    - Write `raw[2]` to the `RTC_TIMEZONE_GMTOFFSET` backup register.
    - Write `raw[3]` to the `RTC_TIMEZONE_DST_START` backup register.
    - Write `raw[4]` to the `RTC_TIMEZONE_DST_END` backup register.
- **Output**: No return value.


---
### rtc\_get\_timezone<!-- {{#callable:rtc_get_timezone}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/drivers/rtc_common.c#L93>)

Retrieves timezone information from backup registers and stores it in a `TimezoneInfo` structure.
- **Inputs**:
    - `tzinfo`: A pointer to a `TimezoneInfo` structure where the function will store the retrieved timezone data.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Cast the `tzinfo` pointer to a `uint32_t` pointer named `raw`.
    - Read the timezone abbreviation start from the backup register `RTC_TIMEZONE_ABBR_START` and store it in `raw[0]`.
    - Read the timezone abbreviation end, timezone ID, and DST ID from the backup register `RTC_TIMEZONE_ABBR_END_TZID_DSTID` and store it in `raw[1]`.
    - Read the GMT offset from the backup register `RTC_TIMEZONE_GMTOFFSET` and store it in `raw[2]`.
    - Read the DST start time from the backup register `RTC_TIMEZONE_DST_START` and store it in `raw[3]`.
    - Read the DST end time from the backup register `RTC_TIMEZONE_DST_END` and store it in `raw[4]`.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value; it modifies the `TimezoneInfo` structure pointed to by `tzinfo`.

### rtc\_timezone\_clear<!-- {{#callable:rtc_timezone_clear}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/drivers/rtc_common.c#L103>)

Clears all stored timezone information from the RTC backup registers.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Writes a value of 0 to the `RTC_TIMEZONE_ABBR_START` register to clear the timezone abbreviation.
    - Writes a value of 0 to the `RTC_TIMEZONE_ABBR_END_TZID_DSTID` register to clear the timezone ID and DST ID.
    - Writes a value of 0 to the `RTC_TIMEZONE_GMTOFFSET` register to clear the GMT offset.
    - Writes a value of 0 to the `RTC_TIMEZONE_DST_START` register to clear the DST start time.
    - Writes a value of 0 to the `RTC_TIMEZONE_DST_END` register to clear the DST end time.
- **Output**: No output is returned.


---
### rtc\_get\_timezone\_id<!-- {{#callable:rtc_get_timezone_id}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/drivers/rtc_common.c#L111>)

Retrieves the timezone ID from a specific backup register.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `RTC_ReadBackupRegister` with `RTC_TIMEZONE_ABBR_END_TZID_DSTID` to read a 32-bit value from the backup register.
    - Shifts the read value 16 bits to the right to isolate the upper 16 bits.
    - Applies a bitwise AND operation with `0xFFFF` to extract the timezone ID.
- **Output**: Returns a `uint16_t` representing the timezone ID.


---
### rtc\_is\_timezone\_set<!-- {{#callable:rtc_is_timezone_set}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/drivers/rtc_common.c#L115>)

Checks if the timezone abbreviation is set in the RTC backup register.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `RTC_ReadBackupRegister` with `RTC_TIMEZONE_ABBR_START` to read the backup register value.
    - Returns `true` if the value read is not zero, indicating the timezone abbreviation is set.
- **Output**: A boolean value indicating whether the timezone abbreviation is set.

### rtc\_enable\_backup\_regs<!-- {{#callable:rtc_enable_backup_regs}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/drivers/rtc_common.c#L120>)

Enables access to the backup domain for RTC operations.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the function `pwr_access_backup_domain` with the argument `true` to enable access to the backup domain.
- **Output**: No output is returned.
